title: AMC CEO Says 2025 and 2026 Box Office Will Be 'Roaring Hot'
text: AMC CEO Adam Aron expressed strong optimism about the box office outlook for 2025 and 2026, stating that both years will be roaring hot. Aron said the company could not be more optimistic about the entire 2026 film slate, which could set a record post-pandemic box office total driven by continued robust growth in the second half of the year. The domestic industry box office so far in 2026 is already up about $300 million year over year. Aron thinks it is easy to forecast that the full year number for 2026 could be somewhere between $500 million and $1.2 billion higher than 2025, representing a $200 million increase in the upper range of his outlook provided in February of a $500 million to $1 billion year-over-year increase. AMC posted its strongest Q1 adjusted EBITDA since pre-pandemic 2019 levels, with adjusted EBITDA up $96 million from the previous year, pushed by stronger domestic attendance, improved European performance and rising demand for premium movie formats. CEO Aron predicted a record post-pandemic box office year for 2026, betting that blockbuster releases, Netflix's theater push and longer theatrical windows will fuel a major cinema rebound.
